questionText: A 240V single-phase circuit supplies a 40A continuous load through 10 AWG copper conductors (10,380 CM) at a distance of 150 feet. Using K = 12.9, what is the approximate voltage drop?
explanation: VD = 2 × K × I × D / CM = 2 × 12.9 × 40 × 150 / 10,380 = 154,800 / 10,380 = 14.9V. That's 14.9 / 240 = 6.2%, which exceeds the 3% branch circuit recommendation.
sparkyTip: Always plug into VD = 2KID/CM first, then check if the percentage is under 3% for branch circuits!

questionText: What is the maximum one-way distance for a 208V single-phase, 30A circuit using 8 AWG copper (16,510 CM) to stay within 3% voltage drop? (K = 12.9)
sparkyTip: 208V gives more distance than 120V for the same wire and current.
